name: AWS EKS Fargate Profiles
description: Creates an EKS cluster that runs selected Kubernetes namespaces on AWS Fargate without managing worker nodes.
architectureSummary: Builds an EKS control plane, core AWS-managed add-ons, and Fargate profiles for platform and workload namespaces. This pattern is useful for teams that want Kubernetes APIs without EC2 node lifecycle management.
securityNotes: Pods run in private subnets on Fargate. The Fargate pod execution role only receives the AWS-managed policy required for image pulls and runtime operation. The API endpoint can remain private-only.

inputs.name: project_name
inputs.label: Project Name
inputs.description: Lowercase project name used for resource names.

inputs.name: subnet_ids
inputs.label: Private Subnet IDs
inputs.description: Comma-separated private subnet IDs for Fargate pods.

inputs.name: endpoint_public_access
inputs.label: Public API Endpoint
inputs.description: Whether the Kubernetes API endpoint is public.

inputs.name: workload_namespaces
inputs.label: Workload Namespaces
inputs.description: Comma-separated Kubernetes namespaces that should run on Fargate.

outputs.name: pod_execution_role_arn
outputs.description: IAM role used by Fargate pods.
